blockingIssues.description: The compile-fail snapshot for non-Store children is noisy and coupled to downstream runtime helper bounds instead of a stable derive-time diagnostic about accepted #[store(ref)] shapes and Store-child requirements.

The runtime feature commit leaves an eprintln! side effect in child-resolution code and mixes macro-contract changes from a separate feature, so the implementation scope and runtime surface are not cleanly isolated.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update rust-library-worker guidance to require macro-contract compile-fail reviews to keep diagnostics focused on the derive contract rather than downstream helper trait bounds, so stderr snapshots stay stable when runtime internals evolve.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: nested-store-macro-contract review found core/tests/ui/store/nested_ref_non_store_child.stderr dominated by Crud/ResolveRecordId/Repo helper errors instead of one clear #[store(ref)] contract diagnostic.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit commit-isolation check before finalizing a feature so resumed workers do not commit changes that belong to already-completed sibling features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: nested-store-runtime-single-and-option review found commit 08a62275 included broad macros/src/lib.rs contract changes even though features.json tracks nested-store-macro-contract as a separate completed feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the approved fallback when GitNexus impact or detect-changes commands are unavailable in worker environments, including how to satisfy required impact analysis expectations without the documented tooling.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews recorded that Windows workers could not run the documented GitNexus commands and fell back to git diff/manual inspection, but mission guidance only partially documents that fallback.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a generic note that runtime code should avoid debug stderr output on normal persistence paths.
rejectedObservations.reason: Too ambiguous and not clearly systemic beyond the single eprintln! instance already captured as a blocking feature issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document in library/environment that Windows workers cannot use GitNexus commands.
rejectedObservations.reason: The problem is normative tooling guidance mismatch, which belongs in orchestrator guidance/skills rather than factual library knowledge.
